title: compare_sign_embedding_configs data payload
description: v0.25.0 sign2vec ensemble query tool. For a single query sign, returns the top-K nearest neighbors from each of six (WINDOW, MIN_OCC) configurations — WINDOW ∈ {2, 5, 10} × MIN_OCC ∈ {10, 20} — plus stability metadata (consensus neighbors across all loaded configs, per-config uniqueness counts). Documents that the v0.23 canonical setting (WINDOW=5, MIN_OCC=20) is robust to hyperparameter choice (consensus_top5_signs non-empty) while the configs do produce distinguishable results (unique_to_each_config non-zero somewhere). Companion to v0.23's find_similar_signs — that tool queries the single canonical config; this one runs the ensemble side-by-side. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

query_sign.description: The sign code submitted by the caller, after whitespace trim.

configs.description: One entry per (WINDOW, MIN_OCC) configuration in the ensemble, in the order they were requested (default: w2-m10, w2-m20, w5-m10, w5-m20, w10-m10, w10-m20).

window.description: ±window context width this config was built with.

min_occ.description: Minimum corpus occurrences a sign needed to enter this config's vocab.

loaded.description: True iff the on-disk cache for this config was found and parsed successfully. When false, see warnings[].

query_in_corpus.description: True iff the query sign passes this config's min_occ gate and has an embedding vector. A high-frequency sign typically passes all six configs; a borderline sign may pass only MIN_OCC=10 configs.

neighbors.description: Top-K nearest neighbors in this config's embedding space (empty when loaded=false or query_in_corpus=false).

sign.description: Neighbor sign code (e.g. an ABZ identifier).

cosine.description: Cosine similarity between the query sign's vector and this neighbor's vector in this config. Both vectors are L2-normalized at build time so cosine is a direct dot product.

occurrences.description: Occurrence count of this neighbor in this config's filtered corpus.

consensus_top5_signs.description: Sign codes appearing in the top-K of EVERY config where the query was in-corpus. Sorted by mean cosine across those configs (highest first). A non-empty consensus is the 'robust to hyperparameter choice' signal — these are the signs the v0.23 paper claim rests on.

unique_to_each_config.description: Per-config count of top-K neighbors that don't appear in ANY other config's top-K. Ordered to match configs[]. A non-zero value somewhere is the 'configs are distinguishable' signal — confirms the ensemble isn't redundant.
